title: Route npm through cmd.exe on Windows due to Node CVE-2024-27980 hardening
context: Node's CVE-2024-27980 hardening breaks spawnSync of npm.cmd shim with shell:false, causing EINVAL on Windows and failing four constitution tests plus degrading ten advisory policies to evaluation errors on each commit.
decision: Create platform-specific spawnNpm() wrapper that routes npm through cmd.exe (shell:true) on Windows while keeping POSIX behavior (shell:false). Argv is constrained to fixed flags and lockfile-validated package names, making this trust model safe. Mirrors existing pattern in src/synthesis/provider.ts.
consequences: npm now works on Windows, restoring dependency snapshots and policy evaluations
Introduces platform-specific branching in critical path code (dependency resolution)
Requires maintaining the invariant that npm argv never contains user input or shell-interpretable strings
Two auto-review CLI tests skipped on Windows because fixture uses #!/bin/sh stub incompatible with cmd.exe
alternatives_rejected: Wait for Node.js to resolve CVE-2024-27980 hardening — blocks Windows users now
Use execFileSync or alternative spawn APIs — still blocked by Node hardening
Find npm differently on Windows (e.g., via PATH lookup) — still subject to same CVE hardening
Mark Windows as unsupported for dependency snapshots — abandons platform

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Wait for Node.js to resolve CVE-2024-27980 hardening — blocks Windows users now
Use execFileSync or alternative spawn APIs — still blocked by Node hardening
Find npm differently on Windows (e.g., via PATH lookup) — still subject to same CVE hardening
Mark Windows as unsupported for dependency snapshots — abandons platform
